platform_decisions.content: Plugins follow mandatory two-level directory layout: component dirs (commands/agents/skills/hooks/) at root, manifest in .claude-plugin/
Plugin identity defined by kebab-case name in plugin.json, unique across installed plugins
Component discovery is automatic (no registration): scans commands/, agents/, skills/ subdirs, hooks/hooks.json, .mcp.json at session start
CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT is canonical way to reference files within plugin; hardcoded absolute paths prohibited
Two hooks.json formats: plugin format (wrapped in description+hooks) vs user settings format (events at top level); wrong format causes silent failure
Hook execution is parallel by default; hooks cannot see each other's output; must be independent units
Hooks loaded at session start only; changes require Claude Code restart (no hot-reload for hooks.json)
Two hook types: command (deterministic, 60s default) and prompt (LLM-driven, 30s default); prompt hooks only fully supported on Stop/SubagentStop/UserPromptSubmit/PreToolUse
Exit code semantics: 0=success, 2=blocking error (stderr fed to Claude), other non-zero=non-blocking error
Hook stdin JSON has common fields (session_id, transcript_path, cwd, permission_mode, hook_event_name) plus event-specific fields
SessionStart hooks can persist env vars via $CLAUDE_ENV_FILE; only hook event with this capability
Hook matchers are tool name patterns only (not content); support exact, pipe-separated, wildcard, regex
Skills use three-level progressive disclosure: YAML frontmatter (~100 words always in context), SKILL.md body (loaded on trigger), bundled resources (loaded as needed)
Skill description uses third-person with specific quoted trigger phrases for activation matching
Skill body uses imperative form; descriptions use third-person — different requirements per section
Skills have three resource categories: scripts/ (executable), references/ (docs), assets/ (output templates/images)
Slash commands are Markdown directives TO Claude, auto-namespaced as (plugin:name) in /help
Command frontmatter: description, allowed-tools, model, argument-hint, disable-model-invocation
Commands support $ARGUMENTS, $1/$2/$3, @filepath inclusion, and !`bash` inline execution
Agent files require name/description/model/color frontmatter; description needs <example> blocks for triggering
Agent triggering relies on description <example> blocks; 2-4 examples recommended for reliability
Agent tools default to all if omitted; explicit listing applies least-privilege principle
MCP config in .mcp.json (recommended) or inline mcpServers in plugin.json
Four MCP server types: stdio (local), SSE (OAuth cloud), HTTP (token auth), WebSocket (bidirectional)
MCP tool names follow mcp__plugin_<plugin>_<server>__<tool> convention; explicit pre-allow preferred over wildcard
SSE MCP has auto OAuth; HTTP/WS use env var token references; hardcoded tokens prohibited
Per-project config via .claude/plugin-name.local.md (YAML frontmatter + markdown body); user-local, gitignored
YAML frontmatter in .local.md parsed with sed in bash hooks; enabled:true/false is canonical toggle pattern
Plugin dev follows 8-phase workflow: Discovery→Planning→Design→Structure→Implementation→Validation→Testing→Documentation
Plugin validation tooling: validate-hook-schema.sh, test-hook.sh, hook-linter.sh, validate-agent.sh
Plugins use semver with pre-release suffixes; default 0.1.0; custom paths supplement (not replace) defaults
Three deployment modes: marketplace install, --plugin-dir local dev, .claude-plugin/ project-local
Marketplace distribution via claude-code-marketplace repo; fork/PR workflow for contribution
Temporarily-active hooks use flag-file pattern to avoid hooks.json restart requirement
Multi-component workflows compose agents/skills/commands/hooks; commands document coordination
Security-first: HTTPS required for MCP, credentials via env vars only, input validation mandatory, least-privilege tools
State sharing between sequential events via temp files with PID isolation; parallel hooks are independent
Agent prompts use second person ('You are...') with Core Responsibilities/Analysis/Quality/Output sections; under 10k chars
Plugin skills distributed as part of plugin package; skill versioning tied to plugin versioning
Plugin paths must be relative with ./ prefix; no ../ traversal, no absolute paths, no backslashes
Plugin validator checks 10 categories: manifest, structure, commands, agents, skills, hooks, MCP, files, security, naming
Hook max timeout 600s; defaults 60s command / 30s prompt; warnings below 5s or above 600s
AI-assisted agent generation returns JSON (identifier/whenToUse/systemPrompt) converted to agent file format
Marketplace plugins require cross-platform compatibility, dependency docs, graceful degradation, namespaced names
Hook timeout validation: numeric only, max 600s, min 5s with warnings; prompt hooks warn on unsupported events
